response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist with genuine geospatial domain exposure (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and past low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec, C/C++ at National Instruments). However, his C++ work dates to 2015, he lists no Rust, and his recent roles (2020–present) are TypeScript/Node.js product engineering. He has no direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tiling pipeline experience, which are core to this role. The systems fundamentals and geospatial interest make him worth a conversation, but the primary-language mismatch is significant.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency distributed systems background (async I/O, caching, high-throughput services in DLang)
Broad full-stack versatility with strong testing/craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No commercial C++ experience since 2015 and no Rust exposure
No routing, navigation, or map-tiling pipeline experience
Recent focus is web/product engineering in TypeScript, not systems-level data pipelines

key_factors.factor: Stale C++ / no Rust vs. primary language requirement
Geospatial platform and GIS domain experience
